1.1: And YHWH spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai, in the Tent of Meeting, on the first of the second month, in the second year of their going out from the land of Egypt, saying,
1.2: “Take a census of all the congregation of the sons of Israel by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names—every male by their polls.
1.3: From a son of twenty years and above, all who can go to war in Israel, you? shall number them by their armies—you and Aaron;
1.4: and with you? there shall be a man of every tribe; each one is the head of the house of his father.
1.5: And these are the names of the men who shall stand with you?: From Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur.
1.6: From Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
1.7: From Judah, Nahshon son of Amminadab.
1.8: From Issachar, Nethanel son of Zuar.
1.9: From Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon.
1.10: From the sons of Joseph: From Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud, from Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
1.11: From Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni.
1.12: From Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
1.13: From Asher, Pagiel son of Ocran.
1.14: From Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel.
1.15: From Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan.”
1.16: These were chosen from the congregation, leaders of the tribes of their fathers; they were the heads of thousands in Israel.
1.17: And Moses took Aaron and these men who had been mentioned by name,
1.18: and they assembled all the congregation on the first of the second month, and they recited their ancestry by clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names, from a son of twenty years and above, by their polls,
1.19: as YHWH had commanded Moses. And he numbered them in the Wilderness of Sinai.
1.20: And the sons of Reuben oldest son of Israel—their genealogies by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names individually, every male from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host—
1.21: those numbered of the tribe of Reuben were six and forty thousand and five hundred.
1.22: From the sons of Simeon, according to their genealogies, by their clans, by the house of their fathers, numbered by the number of names individually, every male from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host,
1.23: those numbered of the tribe of Simeon were nine and fifty thousand and three hundred.
1.24: From the sons of Gad, their genealogies, by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host,
1.25: those numbered of the tribe of Gad were five and forty thousand and six hundred and fifty.
1.26: From the sons of Judah, their genealogies, by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host,
1.27: those numbered of the tribe of Judah were four and seventy thousand and six hundred.
1.28: From the sons of Issachar, their genealogies, by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host,
1.29: those numbered of the tribe of Issachar were four and fifty thousand and four hundred.
1.30: From the sons of Zebulun, their genealogies, by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host,
1.31: those numbered of the tribe of Zebulun were seven and fifty thousand and four hundred.
1.32: From the sons of Joseph, the sons of Ephraim, their genealogies, by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host,
1.33: those numbered of the tribe of Ephraim were forty thousand and five hundred.
1.34: From the sons of Manasseh, their genealogies, by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host,
1.35: those numbered of the tribe of Manasseh were two and thirty thousand and two hundred.
1.36: From the sons of Benjamin, their genealogies, by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host,
1.37: those numbered of the tribe of Benjamin were five and thirty thousand and four hundred.
1.38: From the sons of Dan, their genealogies, by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host,
1.39: those numbered of the tribe of Dan were two and sixty thousand and seven hundred.
1.40: From the sons of Asher, their genealogies, by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host,
1.41: those numbered of the tribe of Asher were one and forty thousand and five hundred.
1.42: From the sons of Naphtali, their genealogies, by their clans, by the house of their fathers, by the number of names, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host,
1.43: those numbered of the tribe of Naphtali were three and fifty thousand and four hundred.
1.44: These are the ones who were numbered, whom Moses numbered—and Aaron and the princes of Israel, twelve men—each one was for the house of his father.
1.45: And all who were numbered of the sons of Israel, by the house of their fathers, from a son of twenty years and above, everyone going out to the host in Israel,
1.46: even all who were numbered were six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.
1.47: And the Levites by the tribe of their fathers were not numbered in their midst,
1.48: for YHWH had spoken to Moses, saying,
1.49: “Only the tribe of Levi you shall not number, and a census of them you shall not take in the midst of the sons of Israel.
1.50: And you shall appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the Testimony, and over all its furnishings, and over all things that belong to it. They shall carry the tabernacle and all its furnishings, and they shall attend to it, and around the tabernacle they shall camp.
1.51: And in the going forward of the tabernacle, the Levites shall take it down. And in the encamping of the tabernacle, the Levites shall set it up. And the outsider who comes near shall be put to death.
1.52: And the sons of Israel shall encamp, each man by his own camp, and each man by his own standard, by their hosts.
1.53: And the Levites shall camp around the tabernacle of the Testimony, that there may not be wrath on the congregation of the sons of Israel. And the Levites shall keep the charge of the tabernacle of the Testimony.”
1.54: And the sons of Israel did according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses; so they did.

2.1: And YHWH spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
2.2: “Each man under his own standard, beside the emblems of the house of his father, the sons of Israel shall camp; some distance from the Tent of Meeting they shall camp.
2.3: And the ones camping on the east side, toward the rising of the sun, of the standard of the camp of Judah by their hosts: And the leader of the sons of Judah is Nahshon son of Amminadab,
2.4: and his host was numbered at four and seventy thousand and six hundred.
2.5: And the ones camping next to him are the tribe of Issachar, and the leader of the sons of Issachar is Nethanel son of Zuar.
2.6: And his army was numbered at four and fifty thousand and four hundred.
2.7: Then the tribe of Zebulun, and the leader of the sons of Zebulun is Eliab son of Helon.
2.8: And his army was numbered at seven and fifty thousand and four hundred.
2.9: All who were numbered of the forces with Judah were a hundred thousand and eighty thousand and six thousand and four hundred, according to their armies. They shall set out first.
2.10: The standard of the forces with Reuben is on the south side, according to their armies: And the leader of the sons of Reuben is Elizur son of Shedeur;
2.11: and his army was numbered at six and forty thousand and five hundred.
2.12: And those who camp next to him is the tribe of Simeon, and the leader of the sons of Simeon is Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
2.13: And his army was numbered at nine and fifty thousand and three hundred.
2.14: And the tribe of Gad, and the leader of the sons of Gad is Eliasaph son of Deuel.
2.15: And his army was numbered at five and forty thousand and six hundred and fifty.
2.16: All who were numbered of the forces with Reuben were a hundred thousand and one and fifty thousand and four hundred and fifty, according to their armies. And they shall set out second.
2.17: And the Tent of Meeting with the camp of the Levites shall set out in the middle of the camps; as they camp, so they shall set out, every man in his place, by their standards.
2.18: The standard of the forces with Ephraim, according to their armies, is on the west side: And the leader of the sons of Ephraim is Elishama son of Ammihud,
2.19: and his army was numbered at forty thousand and five hundred.
2.20: And Next to him is the tribe of Manasseh, and the leader of the sons of Manasseh is Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
2.21: And his army was numbered at two and thirty thousand and two hundred.
2.22: And the tribe of Benjamin; and the leader of the sons of Benjamin is Abidan son of Gideoni.
2.23: And his army was numbered at five and thirty thousand and four hundred.
2.24: All who were numbered of the forces with Ephraim were a hundred thousand and eight thousand and a hundred, according to their armies. And they shall be the third to set out.
2.25: The standard of the forces with Dan is on the north side, according to their armies: And the leader of the sons of Dan is Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai;
2.26: and his army was numbered at two and sixty thousand and seven hundred.
2.27: And those who camp next to him are the tribe of Asher, and the leader of the sons of Asher is Pagiel son of Ocran.
2.28: And his army was numbered at one and forty thousand and five hundred.
2.29: And the tribe of Naphtali; and the leader of the sons of Naphtali is Ahira son of Enan.
2.30: And his army was numbered at three and fifty thousand and four hundred.
2.31: All who were numbered of the forces with Dan were a hundred thousand and seven and fifty thousand and six hundred. They shall set out last with their standards.”
2.32: These are the ones who were numbered of the sons of Israel by the houses of their fathers; all who were numbered of the forces according to their armies were six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.
2.33: And the Levites were not numbered in the midst of the sons of Israel, as YHWH commanded Moses.
2.34: And the sons of Israel did according to all that YHWH commanded Moses. And they camped by their standards, and they broke camp, each one by his family, according to the houses of their fathers.

3.1: And these are the records of Aaron and Moses when YHWH spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai:
3.2: And these are the names of the sons of Aaron; the firstborn Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
3.3: These are the names of the sons of Aaron, the anointed priests, whom he filled with strength to minister as priests.
3.4: And Nadab and Abihu died before the face of YHWH, when they offered strange fire before the face of YHWH in the Wilderness of Sinai, and they had no sons. And Eleazar and Ithamar ministered as priests before the face of Aaron their father.
3.5: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
3.6: “Bring near the tribe of Levi and present them before the face of Aaron the priest, that they may serve him.
3.7: And they shall attend to his needs, and the needs of the whole congregation before the face of the Tent of Meeting, to do the work of the tabernacle.
3.8: And they shall attend to all the furnishings of the Tent of Meeting, and to the needs of the sons of Israel, to do the work of the tabernacle.
3.9: And you shall give the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they are wholly given to him from among the sons of Israel.
3.10: And Aaron and his sons you shall appoint, and they shall attend to their priesthood, and the outsider who comes near shall be put to death.”
3.11: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
3.12: “And I, behold, I have taken the Levites from the midst of the sons of Israel, instead of every firstborn who opens the womb among the sons of Israel. And the Levite shall be unto Me,
3.13: because all the firstborn are unto Me. On the day that I struck all the firstborn in the land of Egypt I sanctified to Myself all the firstborn in Israel, from man to beast. Unto Me they shall be; I am YHWH.”
3.14: And YHWH spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai, saying,
3.15: “Number the sons of Levi by the houses of their fathers, by their clans; every male from a son of a month and above you shall number.”
3.16: And Moses numbered them according to the word of YHWH, as he was commanded.
3.17: And these were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari.
3.18: And these are the names of the sons of Gershon by their clans: Libni and Shimei.
3.19: And the sons of Kohath, by their clans: Amram and Izhar, Hebron and Uzziel.
3.20: And the sons of Merari by their clans: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of Levi, by the houses of their fathers.
3.21: From Gershon is the family of the Libnite, and the family of the Shimite; these were the clans of the Gershonite.
3.22: Those numbered by the number of all the males, from a son of a month and above, there were numbered seven thousand and five hundred.
3.23: The clans of the Gershonite behind the tabernacle were to camp westward.
3.24: And the leader of the house of the father of the Gershonite was Eliasaph son of Lael.
3.25: And the duties of the sons of Gershon in the Tent of Meeting was the tabernacle, and the tent with its covering, and the screen of the door of the Tent of Meeting,
3.26: and the hangings of the court, and the screen of the door of the court, which are around the tabernacle and around the altar, and their cords, according to all its service.
3.27: And from Kohath is the family of the Amramite, and the family of the Izharite, and the family of the Hebronite, and the family of the Uzzielite; these were the clans of the Kohathite.
3.28: By the number of all the males, from a son of a month and above, there were eight thousand and six hundred, keeping charge of the sanctuary.
3.29: The clans of the sons of Kohath were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle.
3.30: And the leader of the house of the father of the clans of the Kohathite was Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
3.31: And their duty was the ark, and the table, and the lampstand, and the altars, and the vessels of the sanctuary in which they ministered, and the screen, and all its service.
3.32: And chief of the leaders of the Levite was Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, with oversight of those who kept charge of the sanctuary.
3.33: From Merari is the family of the Mahlite, and the family of the Mushite; these were the clans of Merari.
3.34: And those numbered by the number of all the males, from a son of a month and above, were six thousand and two hundred.
3.35: And the leader of the house of the father of the clans of Merari was Zuriel son of Abihail. They were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
3.36: And the appointed duty of the sons of Merari was the boards of the tabernacle, and its bars, and its pillars, and its bases, and its vessels, and all its service;
3.37: and the pillars of the court all around, and their bases, and their pegs, and their cords.
3.38: And those who were to camp before the face of the tabernacle on the east, before the face of the Tent of Meeting toward the sunrise, were Moses and Aaron and his sons, keeping charge of the sanctuary to meet the needs of the sons of Israel; and the outsider who came near was to be put to death.
3.39: All who were numbered of the Levites, whom Moses and Aaron numbered at the commandment of YHWH, by their clans, all the males from a son of a month and above, were two and twenty thousand.
3.40: And YHWH said to Moses, “Number all the firstborn males of the sons of Israel, from a son of a month and above, and lift up the number of their names.
3.41: And you shall take the Levites for Me—I am YHWH—instead of all the firstborn among the sons of Israel, and the livestock of the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the livestock of the sons of Israel.”
3.42: And Moses numbered, as YHWH commanded him, all the firstborn among the sons of Israel.
3.43: And all the firstborn males by the number of names, from a son of a month and above, of their numbered, were two and twenty thousand three and seventy and two hundred.
3.44: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
3.45: “Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the sons of Israel, and the livestock of the Levites instead of their livestock; and the Levites shall be Mine; I am YHWH.
3.46: And for the redemption of the three and seventy and two hundred being in excess over the Levites of the firstborn of the sons of Israel,
3.47: even you shall take five—five shekels per head; according to the shekel of the sanctuary you shall take them , the shekel being twenty gerahs.
3.48: And you shall give the silver to Aaron and his sons, with which is redeemed of them being in excess.”
3.49: And Moses took the redemption silver from those exceeding over those who were redeemed by the Levites.
3.50: From the firstborn of the sons of Israel he took the silver, five and sixty and three hundred and a thousand shekels , according to the shekel of the sanctuary,
3.51: and Moses gave the silver of their redemption to Aaron and his sons, according to the word of YHWH, as YHWH had commanded Moses.

4.1: And YHWH spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
4.2: “Take a census of the sons of Kohath from the midst of the sons of Levi, by their clans, by the house of their fathers,
4.3: from a son of thirty years and above, and to a son of fifty years, all who enter the service to do the work in the Tent of Meeting.
4.4: This is the service of the sons of Kohath in the Tent of Meeting: The holy of holies;
4.5: and Aaron and his sons shall come in the setting forth of the camp, and they shall take down the veil of the screen, and cover with it the ark of the Testimony.
4.6: And they shall put on it a covering of badger skins, and spread a cloth entirely of blue over it, and they shall insert its poles.
4.7: And on the table of the Presence they shall spread a cloth of blue, and put on it the dishes, and the pans, and the bowls, and the pitchers for the drink offering, and the bread shall continually be on it.
4.8: And they shall spread over them a cloth of scarlet yarn, and cover the same with a covering of badger skins, and they shall insert its poles.
4.9: And they shall take a cloth of blue, and cover the lampstand of the light, and its lamps, and its wick trimmers, and its firepans, and all its oil vessels with which they minister to it.
4.10: And they shall put it and all its vessels in a covering of badger skins, and put it on the carrying pole.
4.11: And over the golden altar they shall spread a cloth of blue, and cover it with a covering of badger skins, and they shall insert its poles.
4.12: And they shall take all the vessels of service, with which they minister in the sanctuary, and put them in a cloth of blue, and cover them with a covering of badger skins, and put them on a carrying beam.
4.13: And they shall take away the ashes from the altar, and spread over it a cloth of purple.
4.14: And they shall put on it all its implements with that they minister there, the firepans, the forks, and the shovels, and the basins, and all the vessels of the altar; and they shall spread on it a covering of badger skins, and insert its poles.
4.15: And when Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the furnishings of the sanctuary, when is set to go the camp, after that the sons of Kohath shall come to carry them ; and they shall not touch unto any holy thing, lest they die. These are the things that are to carry the sons of Kohath in the Tent of Meeting.
4.16: And the appointed duty of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest is the oil for the light, and the incense of spices, and the daily grain offering, and the anointing oil, the oversight of all the tabernacle, and of all that is in it, with the sanctuary and its furnishings.”
4.17: And YHWH spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
4.18: “Do not cut off the tribe of the clans of the Kohathite from the midst of the Levites.
4.19: And this do in regard to them, that they may live and not die, when they approach the holy of holies: Aaron and his sons shall go in and appoint every man to his service and his task;
4.20: they shall not go in to watch the holy things being covered, lest they die.”
4.21: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
4.22: “Take a census of the sons of Gershon, even them, by the house of their fathers, by their clans.
4.23: From a son of thirty years and above, even to a son of fifty years you shall number them, all who enter to perform the service, to do the work in the Tent of Meeting.
4.24: This is the service of the clans of the Gershonite in serving and carrying:
4.25: And they shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle, and the Tent of Meeting with its covering, and the covering of badger skins that is on it, and the screen of the door of the Tent of Meeting,
4.26: and the hangings of the court, and the screen of the door of the gate of the court that is around the tabernacle, and the altar all around, and their cords, and all the furnishings for their service, and all that is made for these things; and they shall serve.
4.27: Upon the appointment of Aaron and his sons shall be all the service of the sons of the Gershonite, all their tasks and all their service; and you? shall appoint to them as their duty all their tasks.
4.28: This is the service of the clans of the sons of the Gershonite in the Tent of Meeting, and their duties are by the hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
4.29: As for the sons of Merari, by their clans, and by the house of their fathers, you shall number them,
4.30: from a son of thirty years and above, and to a son of fifty years, you shall number them, everyone who enters the service, to do the work of the Tent of Meeting.
4.31: And this is what they must carry as all their service for the Tent of Meeting: The boards of the tabernacle, and its bars, and its pillars, and its bases.
4.32: And the pillars around the court, and their bases, and pegs, and cords, with all their furnishings, and all their service; and by name you? shall assign the items of the charge of their burden.
4.33: This is the service of the clans of the sons of Merari, as all their service, for the Tent of Meeting, by the hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.”
4.34: And Moses and Aaron and the leaders of the congregation numbered the sons of the Kohathite by their clans, and by the house of their fathers,
4.35: from a son of thirty years and above and to a son of fifty years, everyone who entered the service, for work in the Tent of Meeting.
4.36: And those numbered by their clans were two thousand seven hundred and fifty.
4.37: These were the ones who were numbered of the clans of the Kohathite, all who might serve in the Tent of Meeting, whom Moses and Aaron numbered, according to the commandment of YHWH, by the hand of Moses.
4.38: And those numbered of the sons of Gershon, by their clans, and by the house of their fathers,
4.39: from a son of thirty years and above and to a son of fifty years, everyone who entered the service, for work in the Tent of Meeting,
4.40: and those numbered by their clans, by the house of their fathers, were two thousand and six hundred and thirty.
4.41: These are those numbered of the clans of the sons of Gershon, of all who might serve in the Tent of Meeting, whom Moses and Aaron numbered according to the commandment of YHWH.
4.42: And those numbered of the clans of the sons of Merari, by their clans, by the house of their fathers,
4.43: from a son of thirty years and above and to a son of fifty years, everyone who entered the service, for work in the Tent of Meeting,
4.44: and those numbered by their clans, were three thousand and two hundred.
4.45: These are those numbered of the clans of the sons of Merari, whom Moses and Aaron numbered, according to the word of YHWH, by the hand of Moses.
4.46: All who were numbered, whom Moses and Aaron and the leaders of Israel numbered of the Levites by their clans, and by the houses of their fathers,
4.47: from a son of thirty years and above and to a son of fifty years, everyone who came to do the work of the service and the work of bearing burdens in the Tent of Meeting,
4.48: even those numbered were eight thousand and five hundred and eighty.
4.49: According to the commandment of YHWH they were numbered by the hand of Moses, man by man according to his service, and according to his task; and they were numbered by him as YHWH commanded Moses.

5.1: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
5.2: “Command the sons of Israel, and they shall put out of the camp every leper, and everyone who has a discharge, and everyone defiled by a corpse.
5.3: From male to female you? shall put out, toward the outside of the camp you? shall put them, that they may not defile their camps in the midst of which I dwell.”
5.4: And the sons of Israel did so, and put them toward the outside of the camp; as YHWH spoke to Moses, so the sons of Israel did.
5.5: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
5.6: “Speak to the sons of Israel, a man or woman, when they do from all the sins of the man, to act unfaithfully—an unfaithfulness—against YHWH, and that soul is guilty,
5.7: and he shall confess the sin that he has committed, and He shall make restitution for his trespass in full, and plus a fifth of it to it, and give it to the one he has done wrong unto him.
5.8: But if the man has no redeemer to whom restitution may be made for the wrong, the restitution for the wrong is unto YHWH, for the priest, in addition to the ram of the atonement, with which atonement is made for him.
5.9: And every contribution of all the holy things of the sons of Israel, which they bring to the priest, unto him shall be.
5.10: And every man’s holy things shall be unto him; whatever any man gives the priest shall be unto him.”
5.11: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
5.12: “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: A man, a man—when his wife goes astray and she acts unfaithfully against him—an unfaithfulness—
5.13: and a man lying with her lies, sowing, and it is hidden from the eyes of her husband, and it is concealed, and she has defiled herself, and there is no witness against her, and she was not caught,
5.14: and if the spirit of jealousy comes upon him and he becomes jealous of his wife, and she has defiled herself; or if a spirit of jealousy comes upon him, and he becomes jealous of his wife although she has not defiled herself,
5.15: then the man shall bring his wife to the priest. And He shall bring the offering required for her, a tenth of an ephah of barley meal. He shall not pour on it oil and not put on it frankincense, because it is a grain offering of jealousy, an offering of memorial, for bringing to remembrance iniquity.
5.16: And the priest shall bring her near and set her before the face of YHWH.
5.17: And the priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessel, and some of the dust that is on the floor of the tabernacle the priest shall take and put it into the water.
5.18: And the priest shall have the woman stand before the face of YHWH, and he shall uncover the head of the woman and put in her hands the offering of the memorial, that is the grain offering of jealousy. And in his hand the priest shall have the bitter water that brings a curse.
5.19: And the priest shall put her under oath and say to the woman, ‘If no man has lain with you, and if you have not gone astray to uncleanness while under your husband, be free from these bitter waters that bring the curse.
5.20: And if you have gone astray while under your husband, and if you have defiled yourself, and some man has lain with you in intercourse, other than your husband’—
5.21: then the priest shall put the woman under the curse of the oath—and the priest shall say to the woman, ‘YHWH will make you an oath and a curse in the midst of your people, when YHWH makes your thigh to rot and your belly to swell.
5.22: And may these waters that cause the curse go into your stomach, and make swell your belly, and your thigh rot.’ And the woman shall say, ‘Amen, Amen.’
5.23: And the priest shall write these oaths in the book, and he shall wipe them off into the bitter water.
5.24: And he shall make the woman drink the bitter waters that bring a curse, and the waters that bring the curse shall enter into her for bitterness.
5.25: And the priest shall take from the hand of the woman the grain offering of jealousy, and he shall wave the offering before the face of YHWH and bring it to the altar.
5.26: And the priest shall take a handful of the offering as its memorial and burn it on the altar, and afterward he shall make the woman drink the water.
5.27: And he shall make her drink the water, and it shall come to pass if she has defiled herself and acted unfaithfully—an unfaithfulness—against her husband, that the water bringing a curse will enter into her for bitterness, and her belly will swell, and her thigh will rot, and the woman will become a curse among her people.
5.28: But if the woman has not defiled herself and she is clean, then she shall be free and she may be sown with seed.
5.29: This is the law of jealousy, when a wife goes astray while under her husband and defiles herself,
5.30: or when the spirit of jealousy comes upon a man, and he becomes jealous of his wife. And he shall have the woman stand before the face of YHWH, and the priest shall apply upon her all this law.
5.31: And the man shall be free from iniquity, and that woman shall bear her iniquity.”

6.1: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
6.2: “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, when a man or woman consecrates an offering, to vow the vow of a Nazirite, to separate himself unto YHWH,
6.3: he shall separate himself from wine and strong drink. He shall not drink vinegar of wine or vinegar of strong drink. And he shall not drink any grape juice, and he shall not eat fresh grapes or raisins.
6.4: All the days of his separation, of all that is produced by the grapevine—from seed and up to skin—he shall not eat.
6.5: All the days of the vow of his separation, no razor shall come upon his head. Until the fulfilling of the days for which he separated himself unto YHWH, he shall be holy; he shall let grow the locks of the hair of his head.
6.6: All the days that he separates himself unto YHWH, a dead body he shall not go near.
6.7: Even for his father or his mother, for his brother or his sister, he shall not make himself unclean when they die, because the separation of his God is on his head.
6.8: All the days of his separation, he shall be holy unto YHWH.
6.9: And if someone actually dies beside him very suddenly, and he defiles his consecrated head, then he shall shave his head on the day of his cleansing; on the seventh day he shall shave it.
6.10: And on the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two sons of a pigeon to the priest, to the door of the Tent of Meeting.
6.11: And the priest shall offer one for a sin offering, and one for a burnt offering, and he shall make atonement upon him from which he sinned regarding the corpse, and he shall sanctify his head that day.
6.12: And He shall consecrate to YHWH the days of his separation, and he shall bring a male lamb, a son of a year, as a guilt offering; and the former days shall be lost, because his separation was defiled.
6.13: And this is the law of the Nazirite on a day fulfilling the days of his separation. He shall be brought to the door of the Tent of Meeting.
6.14: And he shall present his offering to YHWH: One male lamb, a son of a year, unblemished, as a burnt offering, and one ewe lamb, a daughter of a year, unblemished, as a sin offering, and one ram, unblemished, as peace offerings,
6.15: and a basket of unleavened of fine flour, cakes mixed with the oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with the oil, and their grain offerings, and their drink offerings.
6.16: And the priest shall bring them before the face of YHWH and offer his sin offering and his burnt offering.
6.17: And he shall offer the ram as a sacrifice of peace offering to YHWH, with the basket of the unleavened bread ; and the priest shall offer its grain offering and its drink offering.
6.18: And the Nazirite shall shave at the door of the Tent of Meeting the head of his separation, and he shall take the hair of the head of his separation and put it on the fire that is under the sacrifice of the peace offerings.
6.19: And the priest shall take the boiled shoulder of the ram, and one unleavened cake from the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and he shall put them on the palms of the Nazirite, after his shaving his separation.
6.20: And the priest shall wave them as a wave offering before the face of YHWH; they are holy to the priest, together with the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution, and afterward the Nazirite may drink wine.
6.21: This is the law of the Nazirite who vows the offering to YHWH for his separation, apart from whatever else his hand reaches; according to the vow that he takes, so he must do according to the law of his separation.”
6.22: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
6.23: “Speak to Aaron and his sons, saying, this is the way You? shall bless the sons of Israel. Say to them,
6.25: YHWH cause His face to shine upon you, and be gracious to you.
6.26: YHWH lift up His countenance upon you, and give unto you peace.’
6.27: And they shall put My name on the sons of Israel, and I will bless them.”

7.1: And it came to pass on the day Moses had finished setting up the tabernacle that he anointed it and consecrated it and all its furnishings, and the altar and all its vessels. And he anointed them and consecrated them.
7.2: The leaders of Israel, the heads of the houses of their fathers, they were the leaders of the tribes; these stood over the numbered and made an offering.
7.3: And they brought their offering before the face of YHWH, six covered carts and twelve oxen—a cart for every two of the leaders and an ox for each one—and they presented them before the face of the tabernacle.
7.4: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
7.5: “Accept these from them, that they may be used in doing the work of the Tent of Meeting. And you shall give them to the Levites, to every man according to his service.”
7.6: And Moses took the carts and the oxen and gave to them to the Levites.
7.7: Two carts and four oxen he gave to the sons of Gershon, according to their service,
7.8: and four carts and eight oxen he gave to the sons of Merari, according to their service, by the hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
7.9: And to the sons of Kohath he gave none, because the service of the holy things was theirs, which they carried on the shoulder.
7.10: And the leaders offered the dedication for the altar when it was anointed, and the leaders offered their offerings before the face of the altar.
7.11: And YHWH said to Moses, “One the leader for the day—one the leader for the day—they shall offer their offerings for the dedication of the altar.”
7.12: And it came to pass that the one who brought his offering on the first day was Nahshon son of Amminadab, from the tribe of Judah:
7.13: And his offering was one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.14: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.15: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.16: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.17: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Nahshon son of Amminadab.
7.18: On the second day Nethanel son of Zuar, the leader of Issachar, drew near.
7.19: He brought near his offering, one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.20: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.21: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.22: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.23: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Nethanel son of Zuar.
7.24: On the third day, the leader of the sons of Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon:
7.25: His offering was one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.26: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.27: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.28: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.29: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Eliab son of Helon.
7.30: On the fourth day, Elizur son of Shedeur, the leader of the sons of Reuben:
7.31: His offering was one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.32: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.33: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.34: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.35: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Elizur son of Shedeur.
7.36: On the fifth day, the leader of the sons of Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai:
7.37: His offering was one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.38: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.39: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.40: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.41: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
7.42: On the sixth day, the leader of the sons of Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel:
7.43: His offering was one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.44: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.45: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.46: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.47: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Eliasaph son of Deuel.
7.48: On the seventh day, the leader of the sons of Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud:
7.49: His offering was one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.50: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.51: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.52: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.53: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Elishama son of Ammihud.
7.54: On the eighth day, the leader of the sons of Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedah-zur:
7.55: His offering was one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.56: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.57: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.58: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.59: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
7.60: On the ninth day, the leader of the sons of Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni:
7.61: His offering was one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.62: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.63: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.64: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.65: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Abidan son of Gideoni.
7.66: On the tenth day, the leader of the sons of Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai:
7.67: His offering was one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.68: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.69: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.70: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.71: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
7.72: On the eleventh day, the leader of the sons of Asher, Pagiel son of Ocran:
7.73: His offering was one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.74: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.75: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.76: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.77: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Pagiel son of Ocran.
7.78: On the twelfth day, the leader of the sons of Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan:
7.79: His offering was one silver platter, its weight a hundred and thirty shekels , and one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary, the two of them full of fine flour mixed with the oil as a grain offering;
7.80: one gold spoon, ten shekels , full of incense;
7.81: one bull, a son of the herd, one ram, and one male lamb, a son of a year, as a burnt offering;
7.82: one kid of the goats as a sin offering;
7.83: and for a sacrifice of the peace offerings, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs, sons of a year. This was the offering of Ahira son of Enan.
7.84: This was the dedication for the altar, in the day it was anointed, from the leaders of Israel: Twelve silver platters, twelve silver bowls, and twelve gold spoons.
7.85: A hundred and Thirty shekels was the one silver platter, and the one bowl seventy shekels . All the silver of the vessels were two thousand and four hundred shekels , according to the shekel of the sanctuary.
7.86: The twelve gold pans, full of incense, were ten by ten shekels apiece of the dishes, according to the shekel of the sanctuary; all the gold of the spoons were a hundred and twenty shekels .
7.87: All the oxen for the burnt offering were twelve young bulls, twelve rams, twelve male lambs, sons of a year, and with their grain offerings, and twelve kids of the goats as a sin offering.
7.88: And all the oxen for the sacrifice of the peace offerings were twenty-four bulls, sixty rams, sixty male goats, and sixty lambs, sons of a year; this was the dedication for the altar after it was anointed.
7.89: And went Moses into the Tent of Meeting to speak with Him, and he heard the voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat that was on the ark of the Testimony, from between the two cherubim. And He spoke to him.

8.1: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
8.2: “Speak to Aaron and say to him, ‘When you lift up the lamps, the seven lamps shall give light to the front of the face of the lampstand.’”
8.3: And Aaron did so. He lifted up the lamps toward the front face of the lampstand, as YHWH had commanded Moses.
8.4: And this was the workmanship of the lampstand: Hammered gold—from its shaft to its flowers it was hammered work; according to the pattern that YHWH had shown Moses, so he made the lampstand.
8.5: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
8.6: “Take the Levites from the midst of the sons of Israel and cleanse them.
8.7: Thus you shall do to them to cleanse them: Sprinkle on them waters of purification, and let a razor pass over all their flesh, and let them wash their clothes and make themselves clean.
8.8: And let them take a bull, a son of the herd, and its grain offering of fine flour mixed with the oil; and a second bull, a son of the herd, you shall take as a sin offering.
8.9: And you shall bring the Levites before the face of the Tent of Meeting, and you shall gather together all the congregation of the sons of Israel.
8.10: And you shall bring the Levites before the face of YHWH, and the sons of Israel shall lay their hands on the Levites.
8.11: And Aaron shall offer the Levites a wave offering before the face of YHWH from the sons of Israel, that they may perform the service of YHWH.
8.12: And the Levites shall lay their hands on the heads of the young bulls, and you shall offer the one for a sin offering and the one for a burnt offering to YHWH, to make atonement upon the Levites.
8.13: And you shall stand the Levites before the face of Aaron and his sons, and wave them as a wave offering to YHWH.
8.14: And you shall separate the Levites from the midst of the sons of Israel, and the Levites shall be unto Me.
8.15: And after that the Levites shall go in to serve at the Tent of Meeting. And you shall cleanse them and offer them as a wave offering.
8.16: For they are wholly given to Me from the midst of the sons of Israel. Instead of him who opens every womb—the firstborn of all the sons of Israel—I have taken them for Myself.
8.17: For Mine are all the firstborn among the sons of Israel, the man and the beast. On the day that I struck all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I sanctified them to Myself.
8.18: And I have taken the Levites instead of all the firstborn of the sons of Israel.
8.19: And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from the midst of the sons of Israel, to do the work of the sons of Israel in the Tent of Meeting, and to make atonement upon the sons of Israel, and there will be no plague among the sons of Israel at the coming of the sons of Israel to the sanctuary.”
8.20: And Moses and Aaron and all the congregation of the sons of Israel did to the Levites according to all that YHWH commanded Moses concerning the Levites; so the sons of Israel did to them.
8.21: And the Levites cleansed themselves from sin, and they washed their clothes, and Aaron presented them as a wave offering before the face of YHWH, and Aaron made atonement upon them to cleanse them.
8.22: And after that, the Levites went in to do their work in the Tent of Meeting before the face of Aaron and his sons. As YHWH commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so they did to them.
8.23: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
8.24: “This is what is unto the Levites: From a son of five and twenty years and above, one may enter to perform the service in the work of the Tent of Meeting.
8.25: And from a son of fifty years, they must cease from the host of the service, and shall not serve anymore.
8.26: And they may minister with their brothers in the Tent of Meeting to keep the charge, and they shall not do work. Thus you shall do for the Levites in their duties.”

9.1: And YHWH spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai the second year after they had come out of the land of Egypt, in the first month, saying,
9.2: “And let the sons of Israel keep the Passover at its appointed time.
9.3: On the fourteenth day of this month, between the evenings, you? shall keep it at its appointed time. According to all its statutes and ordinances you? shall keep it.”
9.4: And Moses spoke to the sons of Israel, that they should keep the Passover.
9.5: And they kept the Passover in the first month , on the fourteenth day of the month, between the evenings, in the Wilderness of Sinai; according to all that YHWH commanded Moses, so the sons of Israel did.
9.6: And there were men who were defiled by the body of a man, so that they could not keep the Passover on that day. And they came before the face of Moses and Aaron that day.
9.7: And those men said to him, “We are defiled by the body of a man; why are we restrained from presenting the offering of YHWH at its appointed time in the midst of the sons of Israel?”
9.8: And Moses said to them, “Stand still, that I may hear what YHWH commands concerning you?.”
9.9: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
9.10: “Speak to the sons of Israel, saying, ‘If any man is unclean by a body or on a journey far away from you? or your? posterity, he may still keep the Passover of YHWH.
9.11: In the second month, on the fourteenth day, between the evenings they may keep it; with unleavened bread and bitter herbs they shall eat it.
9.12: They shall leave none of it until morning, and a bone of it they shall not break. According to all the ordinances of the Passover, they shall keep it.
9.13: And the man who is clean, and not on a journey, and ceases to keep the Passover, that person shall be cut off from among his people, because he did not bring the offering of YHWH at its appointed time. That man, he shall bear His sin.
9.14: And if a sojourner sojourns with you? and would keep the Passover to YHWH, according to the statute of the Passover and according to its ordinance, so he must do; one ordinance you shall have unto you? and unto the sojourner and unto the native of the land.’”
9.15: And on the day that the tabernacle was raised up, the cloud covered the tabernacle, the Tent of the Testimony. And from the evening it was above the tabernacle, like the appearance of fire, until morning.
9.16: So it was continually: The cloud covered it by day , then the appearance of fire by night.
9.17: And Whenever the cloud was taken up from above the tabernacle, after that the sons of Israel would journey. And in the place where the cloud settled, there the sons of Israel would encamp.
9.18: At the mouth of YHWH the sons of Israel would journey, and at the mouth of YHWH they would camp. All the days that the cloud remained above the tabernacle, they camped.
9.19: And when the cloud continued long above the tabernacle many days, the sons of Israel kept the charge of YHWH and did not journey.
9.20: And it was, when the cloud was a few days above the tabernacle, according to the command of YHWH, that they would remain encamped. And according to the mouth of YHWH they would journey.
9.21: And it was, when the cloud remained only from evening until morning, and the cloud was taken up in the morning, that they would journey. Whether by day or by night, whenever the cloud was taken up, they would journey.
9.22: Whether it was two days or a month or a year that the cloud remained above the tabernacle, remaining over it, the sons of Israel camped and did not journey; and when it was lifted up, they would journey.
9.23: At the mouth of YHWH they remained encamped, and at the mouth of YHWH they journeyed; they kept the charge of YHWH, at the mouth of YHWH by the hand of Moses.

10.1: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
10.2: “Make for yourself two silver trumpets. Of hammered work you shall make them, and you shall make use of them for calling the congregation, and for pulling up the camps.
10.3: And they shall blow both of them, and all the congregation shall gather before you at the door of the Tent of Meeting.
10.4: But if they blow with one, the leaders, the heads of the divisions of Israel, shall gather to you.
10.5: And you? shall blow a short blast, and the camps that lie on the east side shall set out.
10.6: And you? shall blow a second short blast, and the camps that lie on the south side shall set out. A short blast they shall blow to set out.
10.7: And in the assembling of the assembly, you? shall blow, and not sound a short blast.
10.8: And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow the trumpets; and these shall be to you? as an ordinance forever throughout your? generations.
10.9: And when you? go into battle in your? land against the adversary who oppresses you?, you? shall sound a short blast with the trumpets, and you? will be remembered before the face of YHWH your? God, and you? will be saved from your? enemies.
10.10: And in the day of your? gladness, and in your? appointed feasts, and at the beginning of your? months, you? shall blow the trumpets over your? burnt offerings, and over the sacrifices of your? peace offerings, and they shall be for you? a memorial before the face of your? God. I am YHWH your? God.”
10.11: And it came to pass in the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth of the month, that the cloud was taken up from above the tabernacle of the Testimony.
10.12: And the sons of Israel set out on their journeys from the Wilderness of Sinai. And the cloud settled down in the Wilderness of Paran.
10.13: And they set out at the first according to the mouth of YHWH by the hand of Moses.
10.14: And the standard of the camp of the sons of Judah set out at the first, according to their armies, and over their army was Nahshon son of Amminadab.
10.15: And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Issachar was Nethanel son of Zuar.
10.16: And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Zebulun was Eliab son of Helon.
10.17: And the tabernacle was taken down, and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set out, carrying the tabernacle.
10.18: And the standard of the camp of Reuben set out, according to their armies, and over their army was Elizur son of Shedeur.
10.19: And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Simeon was Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
10.20: And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Gad was Eliasaph son of Deuel.
10.21: And the Kohathites set out, carrying the holy things; and the tabernacle was to be prepared for their arrival.
10.22: And the standard of the camp of the sons of Ephraim set out, according to their armies, and over their army was Elishama son of Ammihud.
10.23: And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Manasseh was Gamaliel son of Pedah-zur.
10.24: And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin was Abidan son of Gideoni.
10.25: And the standard of the camp of the sons of Dan set out, the rear guard of all the camps, according to their armies, and over their army was Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
10.26: And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Asher was Pagiel son of Ocran.
10.27: And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali was Ahira son of Enan.
10.28: These were the order of march of the sons of Israel according to their armies, and they began their journey.
10.29: And Moses said to Hobab son of Reuel the Midianite, father-in-law of Moses, “We are setting out for the place of which YHWH said, ‘I will give it to you?.’ Come with us and we will treat you well, for YHWH has promised good things to Israel.”
10.30: And he said to him, “I will not go. But I will depart to my land and to my relatives.”
10.31: And Moses said, “Please do not leave us, for upon thus you know how we are to camp in the wilderness, and you can be our eyes.
10.32: And it shall be, if you go with us, indeed it shall be that whatever good YHWH will do to us, the same we will do to you.”
10.33: And they departed from the mountain of YHWH on a journey of three days, and the ark of the covenant of YHWH went before them for the journey three days to search out for them a resting place.
10.34: And the cloud of YHWH was above them by day, when they set out from the camp.
10.35: And it came to pass in the journeying of the ark that Moses said, “Rise up, O YHWH! And let Your enemies be scattered, and let those who hate You flee from your face.”
10.36: And in its resting he said, “Return, O YHWH, to the myriads, the thousands of Israel.”

11.1: And the people complained of adversity, and it displeased YHWH. For YHWH heard it , and His anger was kindled. And the fire of YHWH burned among them and consumed some in the outskirts of the camp.
11.2: And the people cried out to Moses, and Moses prayed to YHWH, and the fire was quenched.
11.3: And he called the name of that place Taberah, because the fire of YHWH had burned among them.
11.4: And the rabble who were among them had yielded to craving, and the sons of Israel also again wept and said, “Who will let us eat flesh?
11.5: We remember the fish that we ate in Egypt freely, the cucumbers, and the melons, and the leeks, and the onions, and the garlic.
11.6: And our soul is dried up. There is nothing at all, except this manna unto our eyes.”
11.7: And the manna was like coriander seed, and its eye was like the eye of of bdellium.
11.8: The people went about and gathered it , and ground it on millstones, or beat it in the mortar, and cooked it in the pan, and made cakes of it. And its taste was like the taste of the pastry of oil.
11.9: And in the descending of the dew on the camp by night, the manna fell on it.
11.10: And Moses heard the people weeping throughout their families, everyone at the door of his tent, and the anger of YHWH was kindled greatly; and in the eyes of Moses it was evil.
11.11: And Moses said to YHWH, “Why have You afflicted Your servant? And why have I not found favor in Your eyes, that You have laid the burden of all this people on me?
11.12: Did I conceive all this people? Or did I beget them, that You should say to me, ‘Carry them in your bosom, as a guardian carries a nursing child,’ to the land that You swore to their fathers?
11.13: From where to me is flesh to give to all this people? For they weep over me, saying, ‘Give us flesh, that we may eat.’
11.14: I am not able to bear all this people alone, because it is too heavy for me.
11.15: And if You treat me like this, kill me, please, right now—if I have found favor in Your eyes—and do not let me see my wretchedness.”
11.16: And YHWH said to Moses, “Gather to Me seventy men of the elders of Israel, whom you know that they are elders of the people and officers over them, and bring them to the Tent of Meeting, that they may stand there with you.
11.17: And I will come down and talk with you there, and I will take of the Spirit that is upon you, and will put the same upon them. And they shall bear with you the burden of the people, that you may not bear it yourself alone.
11.18: And to the people you shall say, Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow, and you? shall eat flesh, for you? have wept in the ears of YHWH, saying, ‘Who will let us eat flesh, for it was well with us in Egypt.’ And YHWH will give to you? flesh, and you? shall eat.
11.19: You? shall not eat one day, and not two days, and not five days, and not ten days, and not twenty days,
11.20: but for a month of days, until that it comes out of your? nostrils and becomes loathsome to you?, because that you? have despised YHWH who is among you?, and you wept before His face, saying, ‘Why is this that we have come out of Egypt?’”
11.21: And Moses said, “ Are six hundred thousand men on foot, the people whom I am among, and You have said, ‘Flesh I will give them, that they may eat for a month of days.’
11.22: Shall flock and herd be slaughtered for them to provide enough for them? Or shall all the fish of the sea be gathered together for them to provide enough for them?”
11.23: And YHWH said to Moses, “Is the arm of YHWH too short? Now you shall see if My word will come to pass, or not.”
11.24: And Moses went out and spoke to the people the words of YHWH, and he gathered seventy men of the elders of the people and placed them around the tabernacle.
11.25: And YHWH came down in the cloud, and spoke to him, and took of the Spirit that was upon him, and placed it upon the seventy elders. And it came to pass, when the Spirit rested upon them, that they prophesied—and they never did again.
11.26: And two men had remained in the camp—the name of one was Eldad and the name of the second Medad. And the Spirit rested upon them, and they were among those listed who had not gone out to the tabernacle, and they prophesied in the camp.
11.27: And the young man ran and told Moses and said, “Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp.”
11.28: And Joshua son of Nun, Moses’ assistant from his youth, answered and said, “My lord Moses, restrain them.”
11.29: And Moses said to him, “Are you zealous for my sake? If only he would make all people of YHWH prophets, and that YHWH would put His Spirit upon them.”
11.30: And Moses returned to the camp, he and the elders of Israel.
11.31: And a wind went out from YHWH, and it brought quail from the sea and left them near the camp, about a journey of a day on this side, and about a journey of a day on this side, all around the camp, and about two cubits above the surface of the ground.
11.32: And the people stayed up all that day, and all the night, and all the next day, and they gathered the quail. He who had least gathered ten homers. And they spread them out for themselves all around the camp.
11.33: The flesh was still between their teeth, before it was chewed, and the anger of YHWH was kindled against the people, and YHWH struck the people with a very great plague.
11.34: And he called the name of that place Kibroth-hattaavah, because there they buried the people who had craved.
11.35: From Kibroth-hattaavah the people moved to Hazeroth, and camped at Hazeroth.

12.1: And Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses, upon occasion of the Cushite the woman whom he had taken; for he had taken a Cushite woman.
12.2: And they said, “Is it only indeed through Moses that YHWH has spoken? Is it not also through us that He has spoken?” And YHWH heard it .
12.3: And the man Moses was very humble, more than all men who were on the face of the earth.
12.4: And YHWH said suddenly to Moses and Aaron and Miriam, “Come out, you? three, to the Tent of Meeting.” And the three came out.
12.5: And YHWH came down in the pillar of cloud, and stood in the door of the tabernacle, and called Aaron and Miriam; and both of them went forward.
12.6: And He said, “Hear now My words: If there is a prophet among you?, I , YHWH, make Myself known to him in a vision; I speak to him in a dream.
12.7: Not so My servant Moses; in all My house He is faithful.
12.8: I speak with him mouth to mouth, and even plainly and not in riddles; and the form of YHWH he sees. And why were you? not afraid to speak against My servant Moses?”
12.9: And the anger of YHWH was kindled against them, and He departed.
12.10: And the cloud departed from above the Tent, and behold, Miriam was leprous as snow. And Aaron turned toward Miriam, and behold, she was leprous.
12.11: And Aaron said to Moses, “Oh, my lord, please do not lay on us this sin, which we have done foolishly and in which we have sinned.
12.12: Please do not let her be as one dead, who in his coming out from the womb of his mother, half of his flesh is consumed.”
12.13: And Moses cried out to YHWH, saying, “O God, please, please, heal her.”
12.14: And YHWH said to Moses, “And if her father surely had spit in her face, would not she be shamed seven days? Let her be shut seven days outside of the camp, and afterward she may be received.”
12.15: And Miriam was shut outside of the camp seven days, and the people did not journey until the bringing in of Miriam.
12.16: And afterward the people set out from Hazeroth, and they camped in the Wilderness of Paran.

13.1: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
13.2: “Send men, and they shall spy out the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the sons of Israel. One man—you? shall send one man for the tribe of their fathers, each one a leader among them.”
13.3: And Moses sent them from the Wilderness of Paran according to the mouth of YHWH; all the men were heads of the sons of Israel.
13.4: And these were their names: From the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur.
13.5: From the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori.
13.6: From the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh.
13.7: From the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph.
13.8: From the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Nun.
13.9: From the tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu.
13.10: From the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi.
13.11: From a tribe of Joseph (from the tribe of Manasseh), Gaddi son of Susi.
13.12: From the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli.
13.13: From the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael.
13.14: From the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi.
13.15: From the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.
13.16: These are the names of the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land. And Moses called Hoshea son of Nun Joshua.
13.17: And Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan, and said to them, “Go up this way into the Negev, and go up to the mountain.
13.18: And see the land, how it is, and the people dwelling in it, is he strong or weak, is he few or many;
13.19: and how is the land in which he is dwelling, is it good or bad; and how are the cities in which he is dwelling, in camps or in fortifications;
13.20: and how is the land, is it fat or lean, is there wood in it or not. And be of good courage, and bring some of the fruit of the land.” (And the time was the season of the firstfruits of grapes.)
13.21: And they went up and spied out the land, from the Wilderness of Zin as far as Rehob, at the going in to Hamath.
13.22: And they went up through the Negev and came to Hebron, and there were Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai—those born of Anak. And Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.
13.23: And they came to the Valley of Eshcol, and there they cut down a branch with one cluster of grapes, and they carried it on a pole between two of them; and they also brought some of the pomegranates and figs.
13.24: That place was called the Valley of Eshcol, on account of the cluster that the men of Israel cut down there.
13.25: And they returned from spying out the land at the end of forty days.
13.26: And they departed and came back to Moses and Aaron and all the congregation of the sons of Israel in the Wilderness of Paran, at Kadesh. And to all the congregation they brought back to them word and showed them the fruit of the land.
13.27: And they recounted to him and said, “We went to the land where you sent us, and truly it is flowing with milk and honey; and this is its fruit!
13.28: Nevertheless indeed the people who dwell in the land are strong, and the cities are fortified, and very large. And moreover, we saw those born of of Anak there.
13.29: The Amalekite dwell in the land of the Negev; and the Hittite, and the Jebusite, and the Amorite dwell in the hill country; and the Canaanite dwell by the sea and along the banks of the Jordan.”
13.30: And Caleb quieted the people before Moses, and said, “Let us surely go up and take possession of it; for we can certainly prevail in it.”
13.31: And the men who had gone up with him said, “We are not able to go up against the people; for it is stronger than we.”
13.32: And they gave a bad report of the land that they had spied out unto the sons of Israel, saying, “The land that we have gone into as spies is a land that devours its dwellers, and all the people whom we saw in its midst are men of great stature.
13.33: And there we saw the Nephilim, the sons of Anak from the Nephilim! And we were in our own sight like grasshoppers; and so we were in their eyes.”

14.1: And all the congregation lifted up and gave forth their voices, and the people wept that night.
14.2: And all the sons of Israel complained against Moses and Aaron, and the whole congregation said to them, “If only we had died in the land of Egypt, or if only we had died in this wilderness!
14.3: And why has YHWH brought us to this land to fall by the sword, that our wives and children should become victims? Would it not be better for us to return to Egypt?”
14.4: And they said a man to his brother, “Let us set a leader and return to Egypt.”
14.5: And Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before the face of all the assembly of the congregation of the sons of Israel.
14.6: And Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh, who were among those who had spied out the land, tore their clothes,
14.7: and they spoke to all the congregation of the sons of Israel, saying, “The land that we passed through to spy out is a very exceedingly good land.
14.8: And if YHWH delights in us, He will bring us into this land and give it to us, a land that flows with milk and honey.
14.9: Only against YHWH do not rebel—you?—nor fear the people of the land, for they are our bread. Their protection has departed from them, and YHWH is with us. Do not fear them!”
14.10: And all the congregation said to stone them with stones. And the glory of YHWH appeared in the Tent of Meeting before all the sons of Israel.
14.11: And YHWH said to Moses, “How long will this people reject Me, and how long will they not believe in Me, despite all the signs that I have performed among them?
14.12: I will strike them with the pestilence and disinherit them, and I will make of you a nation greater and mightier than they.”
14.13: And Moses said to YHWH, “Then the Egyptians will hear it , for by Your power You brought up this people from among them.
14.14: And they will tell to those dwelling in this land that they have heard that You, O YHWH, are among this people, that eye to eye You are seen, O YHWH, and Your cloud stands above them, and in a pillar of cloud You go before their faces by day, and in a pillar of fire by night.
14.15: And if You kill this people as one man, the nations that have heard of Your fame will speak, saying,
14.16: ‘Because YHWH was not able to bring this people to the land that He swore to give them, therefore He killed them in the wilderness.’
14.17: And now, please, let the power of my Lord be great, as You have spoken, saying,
14.18: ‘YHWH is slow to anger, and abundant in loving devotion, forgiving iniquity and transgression, and He aquits by no means, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the sons on the thirds and on the fourths.’
14.19: Pardon, please, the iniquity of this people according to the greatness of Your loving devotion, and as You have forgiven this people from Egypt even until now.”
14.20: And YHWH said, “I have pardoned according to your word,
14.21: but truly as I live, all the earth shall be filled with the glory of YHWH;
14.22: indeed all these men who have seen My glory and the signs that I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and have put Me to the test these ten times and have not heeded My voice,
14.23: they shall surely not see the land that I swore to their fathers. And any of those who rejected Me shall not see it.
14.24: And My servant Caleb, because he has a different spirit in him and has fully followed Me, I will bring into the land that he went into, and his seed shall inherit it.
14.25: And the Amalekite and the Canaanite are dwelling in the valley; tomorrow turn and move out for yourselves into the wilderness by the Way of the Red Sea.”
14.26: And YHWH spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
14.27: “How long do I bear this evil congregation, they who complain against Me? I have heard the complaints of the sons of Israel, which they make against Me.
14.28: Say to them, as I live, says YHWH, very truly as you? have spoken in My hearing, so will I do to you?.
14.29: In this wilderness your? corpses shall fall, and all your? numbered, according to your? entire number, from a son of twenty years and above, because you? have grumbled against Me.
14.30: By no means shall you? enter into the land, which I lifted up My hand to make you? dwell in it, except for Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.
14.31: And your? little ones, whom you? said would be victims, I will bring in, and they shall know the land that you have despised.
14.32: And as for you?, your? carcasses shall fall in this wilderness.
14.33: And your? sons shall be shepherds wandering in the wilderness forty years, and shall bear the brunt of your? infidelity, until your? carcasses are consumed in the wilderness.
14.34: By the number of the days in which you? spied out the land, forty days—a day for the year, a day for the year—you? shall bear your? guilt forty years, and you? shall know My rejection.
14.35: I, YHWH, have spoken. Surely I will do this to all this evil congregation who are gathered together against Me. In this wilderness they shall be consumed, and there they shall die.”
14.36: And the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land, and who returned and made complain all the congregation against him by bringing a bad report of the land—
14.37: those very men who brought an evil report about the land died by the plague before the face of YHWH.
14.38: And Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh remained alive of those men who went to spy out the land.
14.39: And Moses told these words to all the sons of Israel, and the people mourned greatly.
14.40: And they rose early in the morning and went up to the top of the mountain, saying, “Here we are , and we will go up to the place that YHWH has promised. For we have sinned.”
14.41: And Moses said, “Why is this—you? are passing over the mouth of YHWH? For that will not succeed.
14.42: Do not go up, for YHWH is not among you?, lest you? be struck down before the face of your? enemies.
14.43: For the Amalekite and the Canaanite are there before you?, and you? shall fall by the sword, for upon thus you? have turned away from YHWH, and YHWH will not be with you?.”
14.44: And they presumed to go up unto the top of the hill; and neither the ark of the covenant of YHWH, nor Moses, departed from the midst of the camp.
14.45: And the Amalekite and the Canaanite who dwelt in that mountain came down and attacked them, and drove them back as far as Hormah.

15.1: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
15.2: “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: When you? enter into the land you? are to inhabit, which I am giving to you?,
15.3: and you? make a fire offering to YHWH, a burnt offering, or a sacrifice to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering, or in your? appointed feasts, to make a pleasing aroma to YHWH, from the herd or from the flock,
15.4: then he who is bringing near his offering to YHWH shall bring a grain offering of fine flour, a tenth of an ephah , mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil.
15.5: And wine as a drink offering, a fourth of a hin you shall prepare with the burnt offering or the sacrifice, for the one lamb.
15.6: Or for a ram you shall prepare as a grain offering fine flour, two tenths of an ephah , mixed with the oil, a third of a hin.
15.7: And wine as a drink offering, a third of a hin, you shall offer, a pleasing aroma to YHWH.
15.8: And when you prepare a son of the herd as a burnt offering, or as a sacrifice to fulfill a vow, or as peace offerings to YHWH,
15.9: then shall be offered with a son of the herd a grain offering of fine flour three tenths of an ephah , mixed with the oil, half a hin.
15.10: And you shall bring wine as the drink offering, and half a hin as a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to YHWH.
15.11: Thus it shall be done for one bull, or for one ram, or for a lamb of the sheep or young goat.
15.12: By the number that you? prepare, so you? shall do for each one according to their number.
15.13: All the native shall do in this manner these things in presenting a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to YHWH.
15.14: And if a sojourner sojourns with you?, or whoever is in your? midst throughout your? generations, and he would present a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to YHWH—as you? do, so shall he do.
15.15: One ordinance is for you? of the assembly and for the sojourner who is sojourning, an everlasting ordinance throughout your? generations. As you? are, shall be as the sojourner before the face of YHWH.
15.16: One law and one ordinance shall be for you? and for the sojourner who is sojourning with you?.”
15.17: And YHWH again spoke to Moses, saying,
15.18: “Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: When you? come into the land to which I bring you?,
15.19: then it will be that when you? eat of the bread of the land, you? shall offer up a contribution to YHWH.
15.20: From the first of your? ground meal you? shall offer up a cake as a contribution; as a contribution of the threshing floor, so shall you? offer it up.
15.21: Of the first of your? ground meal you? shall give to YHWH a contribution throughout your? generations.
15.22: And if you? err and do not observe all these commandments that YHWH has spoken to Moses,
15.23: all that YHWH has commanded to you? by the hand of Moses, from the day that YHWH commanded and onward throughout your? generations—
15.24: then it will be, if away from the eyes of the congregation it is committed in error, that all the congregation shall offer one bull, a son of the herd, as a burnt offering, for a pleasing aroma to YHWH, and with its grain offering and its drink offering according to the ordinance, and one kid of the goats for a sin offering.
15.25: And the priest shall make atonement upon the whole congregation of the sons of Israel, and it shall be forgiven for them, for it was an error. And they shall bring their offerings, a fire offering to YHWH, and their sin offering, before the face of YHWH for their error.
15.26: And it shall be forgiven all the congregation of the sons of Israel, and the sojourner who is sojourning in their midst, because all the people did it in error.
15.27: And if one person sins in error, he shall bring a female goat, a daughter of a year, as a sin offering.
15.28: And the priest shall make atonement upon the soul, the one who is erring when he sins in error before the face of YHWH, to make atonement upon him, and it shall be forgiven for him.
15.29: The native among the sons of Israel and for the sojourner who is sojourning in their midst, there shall be one law for you?, for the one who sins in error.
15.30: And the soul who does beguile by hand, from the native and from the sojourner, he reviles YHWH; that person shall be cut off from among his people.
15.31: Because he has despised the word of YHWH and has broken His commandment, that person shall surely be cut off; his guilt is upon him.”
15.32: And while the sons of Israel were in the wilderness, they found a man gathering sticks on the Sabbath day.
